design_philosophy.core_loop: Vertically scrolling shooter with forced forward movement; pilot ship through enemy gauntlet, manage weapon/shield upgrades between levels, collect story messages, branch narrative path selection
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (credits for upgrades vs. immediate power needs) balanced against bullet-hell execution; branching story creates replay-to-see-alternate-paths tension
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Skilled combat pilot avenging fallen friend against megacorporation; customizing ship loadout to suit playstyle
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Homage to Compile's Zanac series; dual-mode design (story-driven campaign with economy vs. arcade-action with powerup pickup); forces meaningful upgrade decisions; branching narrative in shmup genre is unusual; message-cube story delivery respects player agency (optional collection)

difficulty.lives_system: Shield/armor hit points system (regenerating shields + armor absorption); shields regenerate over time between hits
difficulty.continues_system: Story mode: credit-based economy allows mid-run upgrades. Arcade mode: traditional extra lives pickups. Hidden difficulty tiers (Impossible, Suicide, Lord of the Game) unlock after completion
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based; story mode allows shop/upgrade access between levels
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulties (Easy, Medium, Hard) plus three hidden unlockable difficulties. Hard difficulty gates certain levels and removes line-of-sight for enemies, creating asymmetric challenge. Password system allows replaying at higher difficulty without restart

progression.upgrade_system: Front/rear weapon upgrade (exponential cost scaling up to 10 levels); shield/armor/generator/maneuverability improvements; sidekick companions (some with limited ammo requiring manual activation); shops vary inventory by location but all can upgrade any component; dual-mode customization (story mode: purchased; arcade mode: pickups)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story mode completion grants password for hidden ships and unlocks higher difficulty replays; certain levels exclusive to hard difficulty; hidden difficulties (Impossible, Suicide, Lord of the Game) require prior completion
replayability_hooks: Branching narrative paths force multiple playthroughs to see all story branches
Hidden difficulty tiers with exclusive content
Arcade mode with two-player modem connectivity
Multiple game modes (Full Story, Arcade, Timed Battle, Super Arcade, Super Tyrian)
Different shop inventories by location incentivize alternate weapon builds
Line-of-sight enemy vision system on hard difficulty alters level strategy

world_building_techniques: Message cubes collected and read between levels (some readily available, some require destroying specific enemies in prior level)
Opening cutscene establishes protagonist Trent Hawkins and inciting incident (friend Buce Quesillac killed by MicroSol drone)
Branching narrative paths with player choice at story junctures
Incidental world-building through message cube content (sparse but accessible to engaged players)
Setting context: year 20,031, planet Tyrian, free world Savara, MicroSol megacorporation antagonist, Gravitium MacGuffin

boss_design.philosophy: Variety-focused; many boss encounters with fixed and/or indestructible obstacles requiring environmental navigation; enemies employ fixed attack patterns and stage-specific hazards

danger_signals: Drone assassination of Buce Quesillac (corporate suppression of knowledge about Gravitium)
Trent placed on MicroSol hit list for witnessing murder
environmental_storytelling: Savara established as 'free world' (implicit contrast to corporate control)
Gravitium presence drives corporate interest and conflict
